provisions.title: The purpose of this sub-decree is to regulate the water pollution control in
provisions.content:
order to prevent and reduce the water pollution of the public water areas so
that the protection of human health and the conservation of bio-diversity
should be ensured.

provisions.title: This sub-decree applied to all sources of pollution and all activities that
provisions.content: cause pollution of the public water areas.

provisions.title: Technical terms used in this sub-decree shall have the meaning ascribed
provisions.content:
there to:
a- Public water areas refers to water areas that are for public use such as:
tonle, strung (river), stream, gully, lake, pond, well, sea, peam (river
mouth) and include canal irrigation system and other waterways that are
for public use and ground water;

b- Source of pollution refers to any type of places such as dwelling house,
public administrative building, premise, transport facilities, business
areas or service places from which effluent, pollutants or hazardous
substances are directly or indirectly discharged into public water areas
or public drainage systems;

c- Wastewater refers to water discharged from any source of pollution into
public areas or public drainage systems either it is treated or untreated;

d- Sewage refers to a contaminated water discharged from dwelling and
public buildings;

e- Solid water refers to disable used substance or objects that are disposed
of from pollution source;

f- Garbage refers to disable use substances or objects that are disposed of
from dwellings and public buildings;

g- Pollutant refers to solid or liquid or gaseous substances or all kind of
wastes that cause any charges of component or characteristic of water
such as physical, chemical or biological when it is intentionally or
unintentionally released into the water (public water areas);

2

Unofficial Translation

h- Pollution load refers to the load or the content of pollutant and heat
containing in wastewater released from any sources of pollution into
public water areas or public drainage systems;

i- Hazardous substances refers to any substances that cause danger to
living organisms, damage or break down any objects or building or
adversely impact and damage the environment. The type of hazardous
substances are listed in the Annex 1 of this sub-decree.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 2: Provisions on waste and hazardous discharge
provisions.title: Standards for effluent discharge from any sources of pollution shall be
provisions.content: specified in the annex 2 of this sub-decree.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 2: Provisions on waste and hazardous discharge
provisions.title: In the necessary cases or in response to the requirement of each area for the
provisions.content:
purpose of human health protection and conservation of bio-diversity, the
Ministry of Environment shall set up separated standard for effluent
discharge for sources of pollution, that are located around the public water
area.

The separated standard for effluent discharge as mentioned in the first
paragraph of the article 5 of this sub-decree shall be specified by the Prakas
of the Ministry of Environment.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 2: Provisions on waste and hazardous discharge
provisions.title: The discharge of waste water from any sources of pollution that is not
provisions.content: consistent with the standards for effluent discharge as mentioned in the

provisions.chapter: Chapter 2: Provisions on waste and hazardous discharge
provisions.title: and article 5 of this sub-decree shall be strictly prohibited.
provisions.content: and article 5 of this sub-decree shall be strictly prohibited.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 2: Provisions on waste and hazardous discharge
provisions.title: In order to ensure the human health protection and bio-diversity
provisions.content:
conservation, the Ministry of Environment shall establish the standard of
pollution load contained in liquid waste that could be allowed to be
released from any sources of pollution into designated protected public
water areas.

The pollution load standard as mentioned in the paragraph 1 of the article 7
of this sub-decree shall be stated by the Prakas of the Ministry of
Environment.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 2: Provisions on waste and hazardous discharge
provisions.title: The disposal of solid waste or any garbage or hazardous substances into
provisions.content:
public water areas or into public drainage system shall be strictly prohibited

3

Unofficial Translation

provisions.chapter: Chapter 2: Provisions on waste and hazardous discharge
provisions.title: The discharge of sewage from dwelling and public buildings into public
provisions.content:
water areas without passing through public sewerage systems or other
treatment systems shall be strictly prohibited.

provisions.title: The discharge or transport of wastewater from any sources of pollution to
provisions.content:
other places for any purpose is subject to prior permit from the Ministry of
Environment. The application for this permit shall be copied to the
concerned ministries or agencies.

provisions.title: The types of any source of pollution that shall be required to have a permit
provisions.content:
from the Ministry of Environment before discharging or transporting their
effluent to other places as mentioned in the article 10 of this sub-decree
shall be listed in the Annex 3 of this sub-decree and are classified into two
following categories:

a- The source of pollution of category I that are subject to the prior permit
from the Ministry of Environment when the amount of their effluent
exceed ten cubic meter per day (10 M3 /day) but not including the
amount of water volume used for according the engine.

b- The sources of pollution of category II that shall be necessarily required
to apply for the permission from the Ministry of Environment.

provisions.title: Permit requirement for discharge or transportation of effluent to other
provisions.content:
places as stipulated in the article 10 of this sub-decree existing sources of
pollution except any new project of pollution source that environmental
impact assessment report of which has been approved may be exempt from
the requirement to other places.

provisions.title: The owner or responsible person of the pollution source as mentioned in
provisions.content:
the article 11 of this sub-decree that intend to release or transport his/her
effluent to other place shall be apply for permit to the Ministry of
Environment:
-Forty days (40 days), before the beginning of the functioning, for the new
source of pollution located in Phnom Penh, and sixty days (60 days) for the
new source of pollution located in provinces and cities.

4

Unofficial Translation

provisions.title: The effluent discharge or transportation permit from pollution source to
provisions.content:
other places could be provided if the application form meets the required
technical guidelines determined by the Ministry of Environment.

provisions.title: The owner or person responsible for the source of pollution that is holder of
provisions.content:
permit from the Ministry of Environment for discharge or transportation of
effluent discharge system shall reapply for the new permit to the Ministry
of Environment within thirty days (30 days) before beginning the
modification.

provisions.title: Person that take on lease or ownership of course of pollution from the
provisions.content:
previous owner or the previous responsible person who already obtained
the discharge or transportation permit from the Ministry of Environment
shall continue to comply with criteria points described in the application
form that was submitted to the Ministry of Environment.

The new owner or responsible person shall inform the Ministry of
Environment about the lease or such possession within thirty days (30 days)
after taking on lease or ownership.

provisions.title: The permit of the discharge or transportation of effluent that is provided to
provisions.content:
the owner or responsible person of pollution source could be revoked
temporarily or definitively by the Ministry of Environment after
consultation with other concerned ministries or agencies, if they violate
seriously the article 14, 15 and 16 of this sub-decree.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4: Monitoring of the pollution sources
provisions.title: The monitoring on the discharge or transportation of effluent from any
provisions.content: source of pollution is the responsibility of the Ministry of Environment.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4: Monitoring of the pollution sources
provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment shall take sample at every discharge point of
provisions.content:
pollution sources. The owner or responsible person of pollution sources
shall collaborate with and facilitate the environmental official to take
sample while carrying out their technical task.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4: Monitoring of the pollution sources
provisions.title: The analysis of effluent samples taken from any pollution sources during
provisions.content:
the monitoring or inspection shall be done in the Laboratory of the Ministry
of Environment.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4: Monitoring of the pollution sources
provisions.title: The owner or responsible person of pollution sources shall bear the cost of
provisions.content:
the analysis of his/her own wastewater sample following the tariff
5

Unofficial Translation

determined by the Ministry of Environment and the Ministry of Economy
and Finance. This income shall be included into the national budget in
order to allocate to the Environmental Endowment Fund Account.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4: Monitoring of the pollution sources
provisions.title: The owner or responsible person of pollution sources may ask to have
provisions.content:
his/her effluent sample tested in other public and private laboratories which
are recognized normally and such laboratories carry out the same analytical
method those used in the Ministry of Environment.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4: Monitoring of the pollution sources
provisions.title: The owner or responsible person of the pollution sources as stipulated in
provisions.content:
the article 11 of this sub-decree shall:
(a) be responsible for determining the method of the treatment and the
discharge of their effluent so that it responds to the effluent standard as
stipulated in the article 4 and article 5 of this sub-decree as well as the
standard of pollution load as stipulated in the article 7 of this sub-decree;

(b) have enough facilities and means to prevent the pollution of the public
water area when there is eventual danger caused from his/her pollution
source;

(c) hold the responsibility for installing an equipment for measurement of flow,
concentration and amount of pollutant contained in his/her effluent and also
keep the result for record keeping.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4: Monitoring of the pollution sources
provisions.title: Even if it is found out that the discharge of effluent from any pollution
provisions.content:
source do not respond to the effluent from any pollution source do not
respond to the effluent standard as stipulated in the article 4 and article 5 or
is not consistence with the pollution load standard as stipulated in the

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4: Monitoring of the pollution sources
provisions.title: of this sub-decree, the Ministry of Environment shall:
provisions.content:
(a) issue a written order requiring the owner or responsible person of such
pollution source to correct the violation activities immediately within a
specified time period, if that activity has not caused a harmful impact to
human health or an adverse effect to the water quality yet;

(b) issue a written order requiring the owner or responsible person of such
pollution source to stop his/her activities temporarily until the violation is
corrected, if that activities cause an adverse impact to human health and
water quality.

6

Unofficial Translation

provisions.title: The water quality standards of public water areas for the purpose of the
provisions.content:
conservation of the bio-diversity is stipulated in the purpose in the Annex 4
of this sub-degree.

provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment shall regularly control and monitor the
provisions.content:
situation of the water pollution at public water areas throughout the
Kingdom of Cambodia in order to take measure to prevent and reduce the
water pollution in public water areas.

provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment shall manage data relating result of the water
provisions.content:
quality testing and to asses the status of the quality of public water areas
throughout the Kingdom of Cambodia.

provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment shall disseminate publicly the status of the
provisions.content:
water quality and the situation of the pollution of public water areas of the
Kingdom of Cambodia.

provisions.title: Even if it is fount that any public water areas is suffering of pollution which
provisions.content:
could threaten human life or bio-diversity the Ministry of Environment
shall immediately notify the public about this danger and shall take
measure to prevent the water pollution and to restore the water quality of
such public water areas.

provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment’s inspectors, while conducting environmental
provisions.content:
inspection, shall complied with the following procedures:

a- To present his/her identity card and mission letter while entering into
the premise or any site of point source of pollution for conducting
inspection or taking sample or for checking record;

b- Primary record and report of the inspection or sample taking shall be
done at the site of inspection with participating from any witness if
necessary;

7

Unofficial Translation

c- The inspectors may ask question and require the owner of the source of
pollution to provide them with information and other relevant
documents which are used for report making and for evidence;

c- One copy of record or report shall be given to the owner or the
representatives of concerned ministries (who collaborate with) and
other one copy shall be kept at the Ministry of Environment.

provisions.title: Where if there is complaint or report that any source of pollution discharges
provisions.content:
effluent containing substance which cause danger to animal or human
health or public property or causes pollution to any public water areas the
Ministry of Environment, in collaboration with concerned ministries, may
enter the site of this source of pollution and conduct inspection and take
sample for testing.

provisions.title: In the case of serious accident or imminent danger resulting from pollution
provisions.content:
at public water area. The Ministry of Environment shall make urgent
inspection on the above problem and shall inform the concerned ministries
and local authority.

provisions.title: In the case of clear offense that cause water pollution the inspector of the
provisions.content:
Ministry of Environment shall:

A) take statement collect and detain evidence of such offense and make an
administrative fine if the offense has not contaminated seriously water,
human health, animal, plants or public properties yet:

B) Collect and detain evidence of such offense for making statement
and forward the case file to the competent agency, if this violation
causes serious pollution of water or injure to human health, animal,
plants or public properties.

provisions.title: Violations of this sub-decree shall be fined and punished according to the
provisions.content:
articles 20, 21, 22 ,23 and the article 25 of the chapter 9 of the Law on
Environment Protection and Natural Resource Management.

provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment’s official is responsible for making a report
provisions.content:
of prosecution for any person who violate any article of this sub-decree.
The Ministry of Environment shall take legal action against any offense of
this sub-decree.

8

Unofficial Translation

provisions.title: Any environment inspection official or agent who is negligent, fails to pay
provisions.content:
attention to, or fails to comply with the Ministry’s regulations, or conspires
with a violator or facilitates the commission of a violation, shall be subject
to administrative sanction or face prosecution before the court.

provisions.title: Any provision contrary to this sub-decree shall be annulled.
provisions.content: Any provision contrary to this sub-decree shall be annulled.

provisions.title: The minister in charge of the cabinet of the council of Ministers, Minister
provisions.content:
of Environment and concerned institutions shall implement this sub-decree
according to their duty.

provisions.title: This sub-decree shall be in force from the date of its signature.
provisions.content:
9

Unofficial Translation

Annex 1
Type of the hazardous substance

1. Organophalogen compounds and substances which may form such compounds in the
aquatic environment.
2. Organ phosphorous compounds
3. Organotin compounds
4. Substances that possess carcinogenic (cancer causing) properties in or via the aquatic
environment.
5. Mercury and its compounds.
6. Cadmium and its compounds.
7. Persistent mineral oil and hydrocarbons of petroleum origin.
8. Persistent synthetic compounds which may float, remain in suspension or sink and
which may interfere with any use of waters.
9. Radio activated substances
10. Metals and their compounds

Zinc (Zn) Selenium (Se) Tin (Sn) Vanadium (V)
Copper (Cu) Arsenic (As) Barium (Ba) Cobalt (Co)
Nickel (Ni) Antimony (Sb) Beryllium (Be) Tellurium (Te)
Lead (Pb) Titanium (Ti) Uranium (U) Silver (Ag)

11. Toxic or persistent organic compounds of silicon.
12. Inorganic compounds of phosphorous and elemental phosphorous.
13. Non-persistent mineral oils and hydrocarbons of petroleum origin.
14. Cyanides and fluorides
15. Substances which may have an adverse effect on the oxygen balance, particularly
ammonia, and nitrites…etc.

10

Unofficial Translation

• (E) Discharge upstream of supply sources
• (F) Discharge downstream of supply sources
• (A.B.C.D) Classification of Inland water for Philippine
• (1) Sewer
• (2) Watercourse
• (3) Controlled watercourse, mean a watercourse from which potable water supplied by PUB Act.
• (4) Discharge into the water bodies using for sources of domestic water supply
• (5) Discharge into the water bodies using for navigation, irrigation, bathing, aquatic breeding, …etc
• (6) Discharge into specific water bodies but requiring to get the permit from authority agency
• (7) Protected public water area
• (8) Public water area and sewer

Unofficial Translation

19
